message:
feat(core,embed,fsfs): expand metrics evaluation, cached embedder, and runtime

Continue multi-agent development across search infrastructure:
- Expand core metrics evaluation and search quality analysis
- Enhance cached embedder implementation
- Extend FSFS runtime capabilities
- Harden deluxe TUI E2E and filesystem chaos test suites
- Sync beads issue tracker

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -136,6 +136,7 @@
 {"id":"bd-2tv","title":"Implicit Relevance Feedback Loop with Boost Map","description":"Implement an implicit relevance feedback loop that learns from consumer usage patterns to boost or demote documents in future searches. The consumer signals relevance implicitly (clicks, dwell time, selection) and the system maintains a per-document boost map.

## Design

```rust
pub struct FeedbackCollector {
    boost_map: RwLock<HashMap<String, DocumentBoost>>,
    config: FeedbackConfig,
    decay_clock: Instant,
}

pub struct FeedbackConfig {
    pub enabled: bool,               // Default: false (opt-in)
    pub decay_halflife_hours: f64,   // Boost decay half-life (default: 168 = 1 week)
    pub max_boost: f64,              // Maximum boost multiplier (default: 2.0)
    pub min_boost: f64,              // Minimum boost multiplier (default: 0.5)
    pub signal_weights: SignalWeights,
}

pub struct SignalWeights {
    pub click: f64,       // Default: 1.0
    pub dwell_long: f64,  // Default: 2.0 (>30s dwell time)
    pub select: f64,      // Default: 3.0 (explicit selection/use)
    pub skip: f64,        // Default: -0.5 (presented but not clicked)
}

pub struct DocumentBoost {
    pub boost: f64,               // Current multiplicative boost
    pub positive_signals: u32,    // Total positive interactions
    pub negative_signals: u32,    // Total negative interactions
    pub last_signal: Instant,     // For decay computation
}

pub enum FeedbackSignal {
    Click { doc_id: String, query: String, rank: usize },
    Dwell { doc_id: String, duration_secs: f64 },
    Select { doc_id: String, query: String },
    Skip { doc_id: String, query: String, rank: usize },
}
```

## Integration with RRF

- After RRF fusion, multiply each hit's score by its boost: `final_score = rrf_score * boost_map.get(doc_id).unwrap_or(1.0)`
- Boost is applied AFTER fusion but BEFORE limit/offset, so it affects ranking

## Decay Mechanism

- Boosts decay exponentially: `effective_boost = 1.0 + (stored_boost - 1.0) * 2^(-elapsed_hours / halflife)`
- Lazy decay: compute effective boost at query time, not on a timer
- Periodic cleanup: remove entries with effective_boost ≈ 1.0 (±0.01)

## Connection to Bayesian Adaptive Fusion (bd-21g)

- The boost map provides signal data that the Bayesian online learner can use to update RRF K and blend factor
- Specifically: clicked documents provide positive relevance labels, skipped documents provide negative labels
- This creates a virtuous cycle: feedback → better fusion → better results → better feedback

## Why This Matters

Static ranking treats every document the same regardless of how users interact with it. Implicit feedback allows frankensearch to learn from usage patterns and continuously improve result quality. This is especially valuable for cass (coding agent session search) where users repeatedly search for and use the same high-value sessions.

The boost map is intentionally simple (multiplicative boost with decay) rather than a full learning-to-rank system. This keeps it lightweight, interpretable, and easy to debug — important properties for a library component.

## Testing

- Unit: feedback signal updates boost correctly
- Unit: decay computation at various time intervals
- Unit: max_boost and min_boost clamping
- Unit: cleanup removes near-1.0 boosts
- Unit: skip signal reduces boost
- Integration: feedback loop improves ranking over repeated queries
- Integration: boost persistence (serialize/deserialize boost map)
- Integration: interaction with RRF scoring
- Benchmark: boost map lookup overhead per search result","acceptance_criteria":"1. The feature is fully implemented according to this bead design, scope, and referenced files or modules.
2. Integration with dependent components is complete and behavior remains correct across normal and degraded flows.
3. Edge and error cases are explicitly handled with deterministic outcomes and clear reason codes or errors.
4. Unit and integration tests (and e2e where relevant) are added, passing, and include detailed structured logging assertions.
5. Performance and resource expectations in the bead are validated and documented with reproducible evidence.","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-13T22:02:02.581644590Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-14T03:33:52.652654144Z","closed_at":"2026-02-14T03:33:52.652628636Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2tv","depends_on_id":"bd-3946","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T23:23:54.049928216Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2tv","depends_on_id":"bd-3un","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-14T01:21:07.593117885Z","created_by":"PinkCanyon","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2tv","depends_on_id":"bd-3un.20","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T22:02:44.831563140Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2tv","depends_on_id":"bd-3un.5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T22:02:44.942701265Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":300,"issue_id":"bd-2tv","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"REFINEMENT PASS 1: INTERACTION with bd-1do (circuit breaker) - The feedback loops skip signals could potentially inform the circuit breakers improvement_threshold assessment. If users consistently skip quality-tier-promoted results (results that moved up due to Phase 2 refinement), this is evidence that quality tier is not helping. However, this is a FUTURE enhancement, not a blocking dependency. The current design correctly keeps feedback (document-level boosts) and circuit breaking (query-level phase skipping) as separate mechanisms. Also: The RwLock on boost_map is std::sync::RwLock, NOT tokio. This is correct since boost lookups happen synchronously during scoring.","created_at":"2026-02-13T22:06:49Z"},{"id":317,"issue_id":"bd-2tv","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"REFINEMENT PASS 1: TESTING GAPS - Missing test cases: (1) Unit: concurrent feedback signal submission (RwLock contention), (2) Unit: signal_weights with all-zero weights produces no boost change, (3) Unit: boost map with millions of entries -- memory overhead check, (4) Integration: boost map persistence across process restarts (serialize to what format? JSON? bincode?), (5) Integration: interaction with MMR (bd-z3j) -- does boosting a document also affect its MMR diversity penalty? The boost should apply to relevance score, not inter-document similarity.","created_at":"2026-02-13T22:09:11Z"},{"id":332,"issue_id":"bd-2tv","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"REFINEMENT PASS 2: Expanded testing requirements:
- Unit: concurrent signal submission (RwLock correctness under contention)
- Unit: all-zero signal weights → boost stays at 1.0
- Unit: memory scaling — 100K documents with boosts, measure HashMap overhead
- Unit: persistence format — serialize/deserialize boost map to JSON or bincode
- Unit: NaN/infinity guard — malformed signals don't corrupt boost map
- Integration: boost map survives process restart (persistence round-trip)
- Integration: feedback from federated search (bd-2rq) — boosts are per-doc, not per-index
- Benchmark: boost map lookup overhead at 10K, 100K, 1M entries","created_at":"2026-02-13T22:12:24Z"},{"id":339,"issue_id":"bd-2tv","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"REFINEMENT PASS 4 (testing consolidation): The body has 9 tests, pass 1-2 comments expanded significantly. Consolidated authoritative test list:

UNIT TESTS:
- [ ] Feedback signal (Click) updates boost correctly (boost increases)
- [ ] Feedback signal (Skip) reduces boost (negative weight applied)
- [ ] Feedback signal (Dwell long >30s) applies dwell_long weight
- [ ] Feedback signal (Select) applies highest weight
- [ ] Decay computation: boost decays to ~1.0 after many half-lives
- [ ] Decay at exactly one half-life: effective_boost midpoint between stored and 1.0
- [ ] max_boost clamping: many positive signals cannot exceed max_boost (2.0)
- [ ] min_boost clamping: many skip signals cannot go below min_boost (0.5)
- [ ] Cleanup removes near-1.0 boosts (within +/-0.01 threshold)
- [ ] All-zero signal weights produce no boost change (boost stays 1.0)
- [ ] Concurrent signal submission: 100 threads submitting signals, no panic or corruption (RwLock)
- [ ] NaN/infinity in signal duration does not corrupt boost map (guard)
- [ ] Memory: 100K document boosts, measure HashMap overhead (<10MB)
- [ ] Persistence: serialize/deserialize boost map round-trip (bincode or JSON)

INTEGRATION TESTS:
- [ ] Feedback loop improves ranking: submit positive signals, verify boosted doc ranks higher on next search
- [ ] Interaction with RRF: boost applied AFTER fusion but BEFORE limit/offset
- [ ] Persistence across process restart: save boost map, reload, verify boosts preserved
- [ ] Federated search (bd-2rq) interaction: boosts are per-doc-id, not per-index

BENCHMARKS:
- [ ] Boost map lookup overhead at 10K, 100K, 1M entries (should be <1us per lookup)
","created_at":"2026-02-13T22:18:42Z"},{"id":395,"issue_id":"bd-2tv","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"CRATE PLACEMENT: frankensearch-fusion (FeedbackCollector applies boosts after RRF fusion. New file: fusion/src/feedback.rs. The boost map persistence format should use serde for JSON/bincode.)","created_at":"2026-02-13T22:51:10Z"},{"id":399,"issue_id":"bd-2tv","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"DESIGN FIX: Removed hard dependency on bd-21g (Bayesian adaptive fusion).

The feedback loop (bd-2tv) is a standalone feature that collects user signals and maintains a boost map. It does NOT need the Bayesian adaptive fusion system (bd-21g) to function.

The relationship is REVERSED: bd-21g can optionally CONSUME data from bd-2tv boost map to train its Bayesian priors. But bd-2tv works perfectly without bd-21g — it applies simple multiplicative boosts to RRF scores.

Removing this dep prevents a priority inversion (P3 waiting on P2) and allows the feedback loop to be implemented independently. When bd-21g is later implemented, it should look for an available FeedbackCollector and use its signal data as training labels.","created_at":"2026-02-13T22:52:01Z"},{"id":747,"issue_id":"bd-2tv","author":"PinkCanyon","text":"[bd-17dv retrofit] DEP_SEMANTICS: PARENT_CHILD bd-3un (program grouping only). HARD_DEP bd-3un.5 (scored hit types), HARD_DEP bd-3un.20 (post-RRF application point), HARD_DEP bd-3946 (decision/control-plane contract for adaptive ranking governance). SOFT_DEP bd-1do circuit-breaker signals and bd-z3j MMR interaction are integration guidance only (no blocker edges).","created_at":"2026-02-14T01:21:39Z"},{"id":765,"issue_id":"bd-2tv","author":"PinkCanyon","text":"[bd-2l7y baseline-budget] BASELINE_COMPARATOR: neutral ranking (no feedback boost map, boost=1.0). BUDGETED_MODE_DEFAULTS: max_boost_entries=100000, decay_halflife_hours=168, update_batch_size=100, max_memory_mb=32, retry_budget=0. ON_EXHAUSTION: clamp boosts to neutral and skip further updates until memory/queue budget recovers. SUCCESS_THRESHOLDS_AND_STOP_CONDITIONS: require measurable relevance uplift (for example CTR +2% or ndcg +0.01) with no regression >1% on baseline quality metrics; stop if negative drift persists across 3 windows.","created_at":"2026-02-14T03:06:36Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-2u4","title":"Prefix-Optimized Incremental Search Mode","description":"Implement an incremental search mode optimized for as-you-type search experiences. When a user types character by character, each keystroke triggers a new search. Incremental mode reuses work from the previous keystroke to avoid redundant computation.

## Design

```rust
pub struct IncrementalSearcher {
    last_query: Option<String>,
    last_results: Option<Vec<FusedHit>>,
    fast_embedder: Arc<dyn Embedder>,
    config: IncrementalConfig,
}

pub struct IncrementalConfig {
    pub max_latency_ms: u64,         // Target latency per keystroke (default: 50ms)
    pub min_prefix_len: usize,       // Don't search until this many chars (default: 2)
    pub debounce_ms: u64,            // Debounce rapid keystrokes (default: 100ms)
    pub use_hash_embedder: bool,     // Use FNV-1a for speed (default: true for first 3 chars)
    pub refine_after_pause_ms: u64,  // Upgrade to full search after pause (default: 300ms)
}
```

## Strategy Ladder

1. **Prefix 1-2 chars**: Tantivy prefix query only (fastest, <5ms)
2. **Prefix 3-4 chars**: Tantivy prefix + FNV-1a hash embedding (fast, <10ms)
3. **Prefix 5+ chars**: Full hybrid search with fast embedder (potion, <15ms)
4. **After 300ms pause**: Full two-tier search with quality refinement (~150ms)

## Incremental Optimization

- If new query is a prefix extension of last query (e.g., "sea" → "sear" → "search"):
  - Reuse last result set as candidate pool (don't re-scan full index)
  - Only re-rank within candidate pool + new Tantivy prefix matches
  - This gives O(k) instead of O(n) for each keystroke

## Lexical Integration

- Tantivy natively supports prefix queries on `content_prefix` field
- Each keystroke refines the prefix query (additive — can only narrow results)

## Semantic Integration

- Re-embedding per keystroke is too expensive for quality tier
- Fast tier (potion, 0.57ms) is acceptable
- FNV-1a hash embedding (0.07ms) is even better for very short prefixes

## Why This Matters

As-you-type search is the expected UX in modern applications. Without incremental mode, consumers must throttle search calls (losing responsiveness) or accept high latency per keystroke (poor UX). The strategy ladder ensures consistently fast response regardless of query length.

## Testing

- Unit: min_prefix_len enforced (no search for 1 char)
- Unit: prefix extension detection
- Unit: candidate pool reuse (results subset of previous)
- Unit: strategy selection based on prefix length
- Unit: debounce timing
- Integration: simulate typing sequence, verify incremental speedup
- Integration: verify result quality matches non-incremental for completed query
- Benchmark: per-keystroke latency across strategy ladder","acceptance_criteria":"1. The feature is fully implemented according to this bead design, scope, and referenced files or modules.
2. Integration with dependent components is complete and behavior remains correct across normal and degraded flows.
3. Edge and error cases are explicitly handled with deterministic outcomes and clear reason codes or errors.
4. Unit and integration tests (and e2e where relevant) are added, passing, and include detailed structured logging assertions.
5. Performance and resource expectations in the bead are validated and documented with reproducible evidence.","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-13T22:01:18.624497386Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-14T06:02:25.623338073Z","closed_at":"2026-02-14T04:24:59.094253203Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2u4","depends_on_id":"bd-3un","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-14T01:21:07.305145890Z","created_by":"PinkCanyon","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2u4","depends_on_id":"bd-3un.15","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T22:02:36.304274258Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2u4","depends_on_id":"bd-3un.18","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T22:02:36.411429998Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2u4","depends_on_id":"bd-3un.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T22:19:24.203833640Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2u4","depends_on_id":"bd-3un.6","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T22:02:36.515778201Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":310,"issue_id":"bd-2u4","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"REFINEMENT PASS 1: ASYNC MODEL: The debounce_ms and refine_after_pause_ms timers need clarification. If IncrementalSearcher is used within an asupersync context, timers should use asupersync timing, not tokio or std::thread::sleep. However, if this is a synchronous API called by the consumer on each keystroke (consumer manages debouncing), then no async is needed at all. The body seems to describe consumer-side debouncing (the consumer calls search per keystroke with their own timing). Recommend clarifying: IncrementalSearcher is a synchronous struct that the consumer calls per keystroke. Debouncing and pause detection are the consumers responsibility. The strategy ladder selection is based on query length, not timing.","created_at":"2026-02-13T22:07:45Z"},{"id":331,"issue_id":"bd-2u4","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"REFINEMENT PASS 2: Timer ownership clarification:
- IncrementalSearcher is a SYNCHRONOUS struct. It does NOT manage timers internally.
- The consumer is responsible for debouncing (e.g., UI event loop, async sleep, etc.)
- Remove debounce_ms from IncrementalConfig — that's the consumer's responsibility
- Keep refine_after_pause_ms as a HINT: IncrementalSearcher.should_refine(elapsed_since_last_keystroke) returns bool
- The strategy ladder is advisory: IncrementalSearcher.search_incremental(query, strategy_hint) lets the consumer override
- This keeps the library framework-agnostic (no timer runtime dependency, no async required)","created_at":"2026-02-13T22:12:20Z"},{"id":338,"issue_id":"bd-2u4","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"REFINEMENT PASS 4 (testing gaps): Missing edge-case tests:
- [ ] Unit: query is NOT a prefix extension of last query (complete change, e.g., "sea" -> "mountain") -- must do full search, not reuse candidate pool
- [ ] Unit: empty query string after previous non-empty query -- should reset state
- [ ] Unit: Unicode prefix extension (e.g., multi-byte character input) -- verify prefix detection works with UTF-8
- [ ] Unit: query shrinks (backspace, e.g., "search" -> "searc") -- should this reuse candidate pool or re-search? Clarify expected behavior.
- [ ] Unit: strategy selection returns correct strategy for each prefix length bracket (1-2, 3-4, 5+)
- [ ] Unit: min_prefix_len=0 edge case (should it be allowed?)
- [ ] Integration: rapid sequential queries simulating fast typing (10 queries in 100ms) -- verify no state corruption
All existing 8 tests are well-specified. These additions cover state management edge cases critical for incremental search.
","created_at":"2026-02-13T22:18:37Z"},{"id":344,"issue_id":"bd-2u4","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"REFINEMENT PASS 4 (missing dependency fix):
- Added bd-3un.3 (Embedder trait): IncrementalSearcher holds Arc<dyn Embedder> for fast_embedder. The Embedder trait is defined in bd-3un.3.
","created_at":"2026-02-13T22:19:46Z"},{"id":374,"issue_id":"bd-2u4","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"DESIGN FIX: Body/comment reconciliation for timer ownership.

The body shows debounce_ms: u64 and refine_after_pause_ms: u64 in IncrementalConfig. Per the refinement pass 2 clarification, the CORRECT design is:

REMOVE from IncrementalConfig:
- debounce_ms — consumer's responsibility (UI event loop, async timer, etc.)

KEEP but REDEFINE:
- refine_after_pause_ms stays as a HINT, not a timer. The API is:
  pub fn should_refine(&self, elapsed_since_last_search: Duration) -> bool {
      elapsed_since_last_search.as_millis() as u64 >= self.config.refine_after_pause_ms
  }

The consumer calls this method with their own elapsed time tracking and decides whether to invoke full two-tier search. IncrementalSearcher never manages timers internally.

IMPLEMENTATION API (corrected):
  pub struct IncrementalSearcher {
      // ... internal state
  }
  
  impl IncrementalSearcher {
      pub fn search_incremental(&mut self, query: &str) -> Vec<FusedHit>;  // Uses strategy ladder
      pub fn search_full(&mut self, query: &str) -> Vec<FusedHit>;        // Full two-tier search
      pub fn should_refine(&self, elapsed: Duration) -> bool;              // Hint for consumer
      pub fn reset(&mut self);                                              // Clear cached state
  }

This keeps the library framework-agnostic: works with any async runtime, UI framework, or polling loop.","created_at":"2026-02-13T22:50:33Z"},{"id":394,"issue_id":"bd-2u4","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"CRATE PLACEMENT: frankensearch-fusion (IncrementalSearcher orchestrates across embedders and indices. New file: fusion/src/incremental.rs)","created_at":"2026-02-13T22:51:05Z"},{"id":746,"issue_id":"bd-2u4","author":"PinkCanyon","text":"[bd-17dv retrofit] DEP_SEMANTICS: PARENT_CHILD bd-3un (program grouping only). HARD_DEP bd-3un.6 (hash fallback for short prefixes), HARD_DEP bd-3un.3 (Embedder trait), HARD_DEP bd-3un.18 (prefix lexical execution), HARD_DEP bd-3un.15 (semantic candidate retrieval). SOFT_DEP timer/debounce mechanics are consumer-owned and intentionally not dependency edges.","created_at":"2026-02-14T01:21:31Z"},{"id":769,"issue_id":"bd-2u4","author":"PinkCanyon","text":"[bd-2l7y baseline-budget] BASELINE_COMPARATOR: full hybrid search per keystroke without incremental reuse. BUDGETED_MODE_DEFAULTS: min_prefix_len=2, strategy_ladder=prefix(1-2)/hash(3-4)/hybrid(5+), refine_after_pause_ms=300, max_memory_mb=32, retry_budget=0. ON_EXHAUSTION: degrade to lexical-prefix-only mode and suppress expensive semantic refinement until pressure clears. SUCCESS_THRESHOLDS_AND_STOP_CONDITIONS: p95 per-keystroke latency <= 50ms with completed-query relevance parity >= 99% of baseline full search; stop if parity or latency SLO fails.","created_at":"2026-02-14T03:06:46Z"},{"id":798,"issue_id":"bd-2u4","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"PERFORMANCE PROOF-LANE PROTOCOL (bd-bobf gate)

This bead is subject to the bd-bobf performance proof-lane gate. Before any performance-critical change in this bead's implementation may be considered release-ready, the following evidence artifacts must be produced and verified:

1. BASELINE BENCHMARK: Run criterion benchmarks (bd-3un.33) capturing p50/p95/p99 latency, throughput (ops/sec), and peak memory (RSS delta) for the hot path this bead optimizes. Record results in data/perf-evidence/<bead-id>-baseline.json.

2. PROFILE HOTSPOT EVIDENCE: Profile the hot path using cargo flamegraph or perf. Identify top-5 hotspot functions by cumulative time. Record in data/perf-evidence/<bead-id>-hotspots.txt.

3. OPPORTUNITY SCORE: Compute opportunity_score = (baseline_p95 / optimized_p95). Must be >= 2.0 to justify the optimization complexity. If < 2.0, document why the optimization is still warranted (e.g., tail latency improvement, memory reduction).

4. ISOMORPHISM PROOF NOTE: Document that the optimization preserves result ordering, tie-breaking determinism, floating-point reproducibility, and RNG seed behavior. Specifically: "Given identical inputs and configuration, the optimized path produces bit-identical output rankings as the unoptimized path." If not bit-identical (e.g., FP reordering), document the acceptable divergence bound.

5. GOLDEN OUTPUT VERIFICATION: Run the fixture corpus queries (bd-3un.38) through both the baseline and optimized paths. Assert identical result sets (or document bounded divergence). Include rollback command: `git revert <commit>` or feature flag to disable.

STATUS: Evidence collection is deferred until the implementation is merged and benchmarks can be run against real workloads. This comment establishes the required evidence format for release-gate compliance (bd-ehuk).
","created_at":"2026-02-14T06:02:25Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-2ugv","title":"Cross-Epic Contract Sanity: telemetry schema + adapter version lockstep","description":"Ensure host adapters and telemetry schema evolve in lockstep across core/fsfs/ops.

Scope:
- Align bd-2yu.2.1 schema versioning, bd-2yu.5.8 adapter SDK, and host adapter tasks.
- Add contract checks for schema drift, compatibility windows, and deprecation behavior.
- Require conformance evidence for each host integration before rollout.

Deliverable:
- Single cross-epic compatibility contract and validation workflow.","acceptance_criteria":"1. Cross-epic compatibility contract defines schema-version lifecycle, adapter version windows, and deprecation/rollback behavior.
2. Contract links bd-2yu.2.1 telemetry schema, bd-2yu.5.8 adapter SDK, and host-adapter integration tasks with explicit invariants.
3. Validation includes unit compatibility checks, integration conformance tests across at least two host adapters, and e2e drift scenarios with deterministic diagnostics.
4. Contract-violation paths emit structured logs, reason codes, and replay commands for triage.
5. Rollout guidance documents upgrade choreography, backward/forward compatibility expectations, and sign-off checklist.","notes":"Completed contract lockstep hardening for schema/adapter evolution. Implemented deterministic contract diagnostics in core (reason code + severity + replay command), added explicit classify_version_against drift simulation helper, and expanded contract_sanity tests with two-host drift scenarios and replay command assertions. Added docs/cross-epic-telemetry-adapter-lockstep-contract.md with lifecycle/rollback rules, validation workflow, and release sign-off checklist; linked from docs/telemetry-event-taxonomy.md. Validation: cargo test -p frankensearch-core contract_sanity::tests -- --nocapture (18 passed), cargo test -p frankensearch-core host_adapter::tests -- --nocapture (4 passed), cargo check -p frankensearch-core --all-targets (pass), cargo clippy -p frankensearch-core --all-targets -- -D warnings (pass), cargo fmt -- --check on touched core files (pass). Workspace-wide check/clippy/fmt currently fail due unrelated pre-existing errors/format drift in fsfs/durability/index and other files.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"RainyDune","created_at":"2026-02-13T23:22:52.920544296Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-14T06:18:42.545092879Z","closed_at":"2026-02-14T06:18:42.545067071Z","close_reason":"Completed: cross-epic lockstep contract + deterministic diagnostics + validation workflow","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["contracts","cross-epic","telemetry"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2ugv","depends_on_id":"bd-2yu.2.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T23:23:39.134532276Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2ugv","depends_on_id":"bd-2yu.5.8","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T23:23:58.253069579Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2ugv","depends_on_id":"bd-3un.30","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T23:23:58.381017998Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":442,"issue_id":"bd-2ugv","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"RATIONALE BACKFILL: Added explicit contract/test/logging acceptance criteria to prevent schema-adapter drift from remaining a latent cross-epic failure mode.","created_at":"2026-02-13T23:27:59Z"}]}
+{"id":"bd-2v23","title":"Fix fsfs porting spec mapping for results->explainability toggle","description":"Root cause: FsfsShowcasePortingSpec::from_shell maps PaletteIntent::ToggleExplainability under Search as search.toggle_explain, but canonical interaction spec expects this intent on Results routing to explain.toggle_panel with Explainability target. This can break replay/telemetry/consumer wiring for results-surface explainability transitions. Scope: crates/frankensearch-fsfs/src/adapters/tui.rs + tests.","status":"in_progress","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-02-15T00:01:42.749801445Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-15T00:01:42.749801445Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"bd-2w7x","title":"Ship frankensearch-fsfs as a batteries-included standalone search tool","description":"Ship frankensearch as a usable standalone search tool.

## Current State (2026-02-14 assessment)

The core search pipeline is implemented and well-tested:
- 12 workspace crates, ~2,100 passing tests, 0 failures (excluding frankensearch-fsfs)
- 2-tier hybrid search works: potion-128M fast tier (~0.57ms, 256d) + MiniLM-L6-v2 quality tier (~128ms, 384d)
- Lexical search via Tantivy BM25, fused with semantic via RRF (K=60)
- Progressive iterator: fast results immediately, refined results if quality tier completes in time
- Storage layer (FrankenSQLite-backed document store, job queue, content-hash dedup)
- Durability layer (RaptorQ FEC sidecars for corruption detection/repair)
- Ops TUI (ratatui-based fleet monitoring, lifecycle tracking, telemetry dashboards)
- FSVI binary vector index format with f16 quantization and HNSW
- Extensive test coverage across core, fusion, index, embed, lexical, rerank, storage, durability, ops, tui

## Gap Analysis

Despite strong internals, frankensearch cannot be used as a standalone tool today:

1. **frankensearch-fsfs doesn't compile** — serde lifetime error in lifecycle.rs blocks the CLI
2. **No model auto-download** — embedder traits expect ONNX models already on disk; no download mechanism
3. **No packaged binary** — no `cargo install` support, no default config for cold-start
4. **CLI not validated end-to-end** — individual modules tested but no proof the full index→search→display pipeline works
5. **No user documentation** — extensive internal docs/beads but no README quick-start or getting-started guide

## Goal

Close the gap so that a developer can: `cargo install frankensearch && frankensearch index ./my-project && frankensearch search "how does auth work"` — and get useful results within 60 seconds of first contact.

## Tiers

- **T0: Fix what's broken** — compile errors, workspace hygiene
- **T1: Model management** — auto-download, caching, integrity
- **T2: End-to-end CLI** — index, search, explain, watch, defaults
- **T3: Packaging** — cargo install, error UX, platform support
- **T4: Documentation** — README, architecture, examples
- **T5: Integration testing** — e2e validation, performance baselines, CI

## Cross-Epic Dependencies

- Depends on bd-3un (CLOSED): core search pipeline
- Depends on bd-3w1: FrankenSQLite storage integration (partially complete)
- Relates to bd-2hz: existing FSFS workstream (this epic subsumes the "ship it" subset)
- Relates to bd-2yu: ops TUI workstream (parallel effort, not blocking)","status":"in_progress","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","assignee":"LavenderBay","created_at":"2026-02-14T17:10:37.300706430Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-14T18:34:35.605032176Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["epic","milestone","ship"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2w7x","depends_on_id":"bd-2hz","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-14T17:18:43.641468457Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":1346,"issue_id":"bd-2w7x","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"## Design Philosophy

This epic follows DCG (Destructive Command Guard) as the gold standard for developer
tool UX. DCG's install.sh is 2117 lines of beautifully crafted bash that handles:
platform detection, agent auto-detection (7 AI agents), SHA-256 verification, gum-based
beautiful output with ANSI fallback, shell completion auto-installation, auto-update
with rollback, and XDG-compliant configuration. We replicate every one of these patterns.

## The 60-Second Rule

A developer should go from zero to useful search results in under 60 seconds:
```bash
curl -fsSL https://frankensearch.dev/install.sh | bash    # 10s (binary download)
fsfs index ./my-project                                    # 30s (auto-downloads models, indexes)
fsfs search "how does auth work"                           # 1s (search with colored results)
```

## Tier Execution Strategy

- **T0 (Fix)**: Do first. Nothing else can proceed until fsfs compiles.
- **T1 (Models)**: Do second. Auto-download is the key enabler for zero-config.
- **T2 (CLI)**: Do third. Wire the actual index/search commands.
- **T3 (Installer)**: Do fourth. The curl-bash experience.
- **T4 (Update)**: Can be deferred. Nice-to-have for v1.
- **T5 (Packaging)**: Partially parallel with T2-T3. cargo install + cross-platform.
- **T6 (Docs)**: Can start anytime. README should be written early for clarity.
- **T7 (Testing)**: Continuous. E2E test is the ship gate.

## Critical Path

bd-2w7x.1 (compile fix) -> bd-2w7x.2 (workspace check) -> bd-2w7x.4 (model cache)
-> bd-2w7x.5 (manifest) -> bd-2w7x.6 (auto-download) -> bd-2w7x.9 (index command)
-> bd-2w7x.10 (search command) -> bd-2w7x.37 (e2e test)

This is the minimum viable path to a working product. Everything else is polish.

## Cross-Epic Dependencies

- **bd-3un (CLOSED)**: Core search pipeline. This epic builds the CLI on top of it.
- **bd-3w1 (partial)**: FrankenSQLite storage. The storage layer is needed for index
  persistence but can use in-memory fallback if not complete.
- **bd-2hz (in progress)**: FSFS feature workstream. This epic subsumes the "ship it"
  portion. bd-2hz continues for advanced features (TUI, performance, testing).
- **bd-2yu (in progress)**: Ops TUI. Parallel effort, not blocking shipping.

## asupersync Constraint (PROJECT-WIDE)

ALL async code must use asupersync. Tokio, reqwest, and hyper are FORBIDDEN.
This affects model download (bd-2w7x.6), update check (bd-2w7x.25), and any
other network operation. See MEMORY.md "Asupersync Integration" section.

## What "Done" Looks Like

1. `cargo install frankensearch-fsfs` works
2. `curl ... | bash` installs the binary
3. `fsfs index . && fsfs search "foo"` returns relevant results
4. Models are auto-downloaded on first use
5. README exists with quick-start instructions
6. CI pipeline passes all tests
7. E2E test demonstrates recall quality
","created_at":"2026-02-14T17:28:15Z"},{"id":1377,"issue_id":"bd-2w7x","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"## Revision: 8 New Beads Added (48 total)

### New Test Beads (4)
- **bd-2w7x.41**: Unit tests for model management (cache, manifest, download, integrity, offline)
- **bd-2w7x.42**: Unit tests for CLI commands (index, search, stream, explain, watch)
- **bd-2w7x.43**: Installer test suite (shellcheck + bats functional tests)
- **bd-2w7x.46**: Unit tests for auto-update (self-update, version cache, rollback)

### New Feature Beads (2)
- **bd-2w7x.45**: `fsfs download-models` explicit command for model cache management
- **bd-2w7x.48**: `fsfs status` command for index health and system state

### New Infrastructure Beads (2)
- **bd-2w7x.44**: Structured CLI tracing/logging (subscriber, verbose modes, log files)
- **bd-2w7x.47**: E2E installer smoke test (full install-index-search-uninstall lifecycle)

### Dependency Fixes Applied
1. REMOVED: bd-2w7x.37 -> bd-2hz.10 (was blocking e2e test on entire testing workstream)
2. ADDED: bd-2w7x.9 -> bd-2w7x.28 (index command needs entry point scaffold)
3. ADDED: bd-2w7x.10 -> bd-2w7x.28 (search command needs entry point scaffold)
4. ADDED: bd-2w7x.16 -> bd-2w7x.17 (installer scaffold uses beautiful output library)
5. ADDED: bd-2w7x.23 -> bd-2w7x.21 (uninstall must clean up shell completions)
6. ADDED: bd-2w7x.37 -> bd-2w7x.44 (e2e test uses tracing for diagnostic logging)

### bv Suggestions Evaluated (10 total)
- 2 ACCEPTED: .23->.21 (uninstall+completions), .16->.17 (installer+output)
- 8 REJECTED as false positives (lexical similarity, not real deps):
  .19->.18 (alternatives, not chain), .20->.19, .23->.19, .23->.22,
  .21->.19, .13->.10, .26->.25 (rollback needs version info — wait, this IS valid)

### Correction: .26 -> .25 also added

 {"id":"bd-2w7x.1","title":"Fix FSFS lifecycle.rs serde lifetime compile error","description":"The frankensearch-fsfs crate fails to compile due to a serde Deserialize lifetime error in lifecycle.rs around the DiskBudgetSnapshot field. The error is: 'lifetime may not live long enough — requires that de must outlive static'. This is likely a missing #[serde(borrow)] or a struct containing owned types that need DeserializeOwned instead of Deserialize<'de>. This is the single blocking issue preventing the entire CLI from compiling. Another concurrent agent introduced this regression. The fix is probably one line — either change the derive to use DeserializeOwned, add a lifetime bound, or restructure the DiskBudgetSnapshot type. Must compile with cargo check --workspace --all-targets before closing.","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"bug","assignee":"QuietAnchor","created_at":"2026-02-14T17:10:51.535009972Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-14T17:19:19.231500412Z","closed_at":"2026-02-14T17:14:41.939842960Z","close_reason":"Already fixed upstream; fsfs crate compiles successfully on verification run","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["blocking","bug","fsfs","t0-fix"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2w7x.1","depends_on_id":"bd-2w7x","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-14T17:10:51.535009972Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":1305,"issue_id":"bd-2w7x.1","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"## Root Cause Analysis

The compile error is in `crates/frankensearch-fsfs/src/lifecycle.rs:179`. The serde
Deserialize derive on DiskBudgetSnapshot has a lifetime conflict where `'de` must
outlive `'static`. This is a known serde pattern issue — happens when:

1. A struct derives `Deserialize<'de>` but contains a field whose type only implements
   `DeserializeOwned` (i.e., `Deserialize<'static>`)
2. A borrowed field (like `&'de str`) appears alongside an owned field that has a
   `'static` bound somewhere in its type hierarchy

## Fix Strategy (pick one)

- **Option A**: Change the derived trait from `Deserialize<'de>` to `DeserializeOwned`
  by adding `#[serde(bound(deserialize = ""))]` on the offending field
- **Option B**: Ensure all inner types use owned strings (`String`) rather than
  borrowed (`&str`) — check DiskBudgetSnapshot and its transitive field types
- **Option C**: Add `#[serde(borrow)]` if the type genuinely borrows from input

## Validation

After fixing, run: `cargo check --workspace --all-targets` — must pass clean.
Then `cargo test -p frankensearch-fsfs` to verify tests still compile and pass.

## Context

This regression was introduced by a concurrent agent writing lifecycle.rs. The entire
frankensearch-fsfs binary crate is blocked on this single issue. All other 11 workspace
crates compile and pass 2,100+ tests. This is the #1 priority blocker for the ship epic.
","created_at":"2026-02-14T17:19:19Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-2w7x.10","title":"fsfs search <query> returns results end-to-end","description":"Validate and fix the complete search pipeline: fsfs search 'how does auth work' should (1) parse the query (ParsedQuery with classification), (2) embed the query using potion-128M, (3) search the FSVI vector index for semantic candidates, (4) search the Tantivy index for lexical candidates, (5) fuse results via RRF, (6) optionally refine with MiniLM-L6-v2 quality embedder, (7) optionally rerank with FlashRank cross-encoder, (8) display results in a human-readable format with file paths, relevance scores, and text snippets. The default output should be pretty-printed and colored (not JSON — that's the --format json mode). Must handle: empty results (helpful message), no index (tell user to run 'fsfs index' first), single-word queries, multi-word natural language queries, identifier queries (camelCase, snake_case). Exit code 0 on success, even if zero results.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","assignee":"EmeraldFrog","created_at":"2026-02-14T17:11:57.300069090Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-14T19:40:58.027124711Z","closed_at":"2026-02-14T19:40:58.027091679Z","close_reason":"Completed runtime search wiring and validation","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["cli","critical","e2e","feature","t2-cli"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2w7x.10","depends_on_id":"bd-2w7x","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-14T17:11:57.300069090Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2w7x.10","depends_on_id":"bd-2w7x.28","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-14T18:29:09.328863142Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2w7x.10","depends_on_id":"bd-2w7x.9","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-14T17:15:55.077613551Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":1315,"issue_id":"bd-2w7x.10","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"## Design: `fsfs search <query>` Command

The core search experience. Must return useful results instantly:

```bash
$ fsfs search "how does authentication work"
  1. src/auth/middleware.rs:45       0.87  JWT validation middleware
  2. src/auth/login.rs:12           0.82  Login handler with password hashing
  3. src/config/security.rs:89      0.74  Security configuration and token settings
  4. docs/auth.md:1                 0.71  Authentication architecture overview
```

## Search Pipeline (existing, just needs wiring)

1. Parse query → `ParsedQuery` (frankensearch-core/parsed_query.rs)
2. Classify query → `QueryClass` (core/query_class.rs) for adaptive budgets
3. Fast-tier semantic search (potion-128M) + BM25 lexical search
4. RRF fusion (K=60) of semantic + lexical results
5. Optional quality-tier refinement (MiniLM-L6-v2) with time budget
6. Display results with snippet highlighting

## Output Format

Default (TTY): colored output with rank, file:line, score, snippet
Pipe mode (non-TTY): tab-separated path:line\tscore\tsnippet (machine-parseable)
JSON mode (--json): full structured output with all metadata

## CLI Interface

```
fsfs search <QUERY> [OPTIONS]
  --index-dir <PATH>     Search index location (default: auto-detect from cwd)
  --limit <N>            Max results (default: 10)
  --format <FMT>         Output format: auto|text|json|toon (default: auto)
  --no-refine            Skip quality tier refinement
  --explain              Include score breakdown (see bd-2w7x.12)
```

## Index Auto-Detection

Search should auto-detect the index by walking up from cwd looking for `.frankensearch/`
directory, similar to how git finds `.git/`. If no index found, suggest running
`fsfs index` first.

## Existing Code

- `frankensearch-fsfs/src/query_execution.rs` — has `QueryExecutionOrchestrator`
- `frankensearch-fsfs/src/query_planning.rs` — has `QueryPlanner` with intent classification
- `frankensearch-fusion/src/searcher.rs` — has `TwoTierSearcher::search()`
- `frankensearch-fsfs/src/output_schema.rs` — has `OutputEnvelope` for structured output
- `frankensearch-fsfs/src/adapters/format_emitter.rs` — has format emission functions
","created_at":"2026-02-14T17:22:26Z"},{"id":1374,"issue_id":"bd-2w7x.10","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"## Revision: Entry Point Dependency Added

bd-2w7x.10 now depends on bd-2w7x.28 (binary entry point). The search command needs
the clap dispatch to exist before it can be wired in.

## Revision: Tracing Integration

Search operations must emit structured tracing for every phase:
- `query_parse` span: raw query, parsed query, query class
- `fast_search` span: semantic results count, lexical results count, latency
- `fusion` span: RRF K, blend factor, fused results count
- `quality_refine` span (optional): quality embeddings, latency, timeout status
- `rerank` span (optional): reranker model, score distribution
- `display` span: format, results rendered, total latency

## Revision: Status Command Integration

The new bd-2w7x.48 (fsfs status) complements this bead. Where search USES the index,
status REPORTS on the index. Both need the entry point scaffold and config defaults.
","created_at":"2026-02-14T18:34:11Z"}]}

Deliverables:

 {"id":"bd-ehuk","title":"Release Gate: advanced ranking/control composition sign-off (bd-3un.52 mandatory)","description":"Hard release gate for advanced ranking/control bundle.

Gate condition:

- Advanced features covered: bd-21g, bd-22k, bd-2ps, bd-2yj, bd-1do, bd-2tv, bd-z3j, bd-3st, bd-2n6, bd-6sj.

Required artifacts:
- Matrix run report with fixture slices and deterministic replay handles.
- Structured logs/metrics assertions for phase transitions, fallback reasons, and ordering invariants.
- Explicit sign-off notes for known limitations and residual risks.","acceptance_criteria":"1. Mandatory dependency beads (including bd-3un.52 and listed advanced-feature components) are complete and green under release-gate verification.
2. Interaction matrix report includes deterministic fixture lanes, replay commands, ordering/fallback invariants, and residual-risk annotations.
3. Unit/integration/e2e gate suites for advanced ranking/control composition pass with unified diagnostic artifact bundles.
4. Sign-off package documents known limitations, approved mitigations, rollback criteria, and release decision authority.

PRIMARY_SURFACES: .beads/issues.jsonl comment payload for bd-ehuk; no source-code behavior changes.","created_at":"2026-02-14T08:21:40Z"},{"id":1016,"issue_id":"bd-ehuk","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"[bd-3qwe self-doc] EVIDENCE
UNIT_TESTS: For bd-ehuk, require deterministic unit coverage for the primary invariants and edge cases touched by the bead scope.
INTEGRATION_TESTS: For bd-ehuk, require cross-component contract validation over declared dependency edges and degraded/error paths.
E2E_TESTS: For bd-ehuk, require user-visible or pipeline-level scenario validation when applicable; otherwise record explicit N/A reason in execution artifacts.
PERFORMANCE_VALIDATION: For bd-ehuk, include baseline comparator and budgeted-mode evidence per bd-2l7y (latency/throughput/resource expectations).
LOGGING_ARTIFACTS: For bd-ehuk, emit structured logs/traces plus reproducible artifact references (manifest path, replay command, CI job link).","created_at":"2026-02-14T08:26:39Z"}]}
